def: v.t. = pūćhnā, q.v.

ety: pūćh˚ = Prk. पुच्छ(इ) = S. पृच्छ(ति), rt. प्रछ
def: v.t. To ask (of, -se), to inquire (of), to question, interrogate; to refer (to), to consult; to ask or inquire after (in this and the following senses pūćhnā governs the dative form of the accusative instead of the ablative); to care (about), feel concern (for); to mind, heed, regard, take notice (of); to invite; to help, assist
more: v. a. 1. To ask; question; put a question; interrogate; enquire; require an answer. 1. Pānī pī-ke zāt pūchhte ho? Prov. First drink his water and then ask his caste! 2. Mere ghoṛe kī kuchh na pūchho! Players' idiom. 3. Pūchhā kisī ne ye kisī kāmil faqīr se, 'Ye mehr o māh Haq ne banāë haiṅ kāhe ke?' Woh sun-ke bolā, Bābā, Khudā tujh-ko khair de, Ham to na chāṅd samjheṅ, na sūraj haiṅ jānte, Bābā, hameṅ to ye nazar ātī haiṅ roṭīyāṅ? ' On bread. Nazīr. A question one did put a perfect saint one day, 'The sun and moon how hath God made, say with what clay? He heard and said, 'Good Sir, God's blessing on your head! Nor sun nor moon I know, all I can see is bread? 4. Pūchhte pūchhte to Dillī pahuṅch jāte haiṅ . Prov. Ask oft your way and you may get to Delhi. (To ask is to learn). 2. To enquire after one's health or welfare. 3. To refer or make a reference to; consult. 4. To account; value; r…
v. a. To ask, to inquire, to question, to interrogate.
ex: میں آپ سے کُچْھ پُوچْھنا چاہتی ہوں.
ma͠i āp se kuch pūchnā cāhtī hū̃.
I would like to ask you something.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀘𑁆𑀙𑀤𑀺 (pucchadi), from Sanskrit पृच्छति (pṛccháti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *pr̥šćáti, from Proto-Indo-European *pr̥sḱéti. Cognate with Gujarati પૂછવું (pūchvũ), Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Kalasha phúčik, Romani pućhel, Persian پرسیدن (porsīdan), German forschen.

v. a. To proclaim publickly, to call.
s.f. Calling out aloud; call, shout, bawl, halloo, noise, din; cry, outcry, summons; invitation; invocation; petition, suit, complaint, plaint; call (for), want or lack (of), need (of).
more: n. f. S. प्रकुश to call out. 1. Call; calling out ( bulāo ); cry for help; halloo. Kyā abr hai, chaman hai, hawā hai, bahār hai, Sāqī pahuṅch shitāb, ki terī pukār hai . Jurat. 2. Noise; din; shout; roar. Hāthī chhūṭā thān se, sāgar paṛī pukār, Sab darwāze band rahe, nikal gayā aswār . The soul. Dohā. But now held hard, broke from his stall—the world with clamour rent— The doors all closed, the rider 'scaped without impediment. 3. A summons; invitation. 4. Invocation; address; prayer. 5. Petition; suit; request ( darkhuāst ). 6. Complaint ( faryād ); plaint. Mālī āyā bāg meṅ, kalyan karī pukār, Phūlī phūlī ṭūṭ gaīṅ, kāl hamārī bār . Dohā. 'The gardener in the garden's come,' the young buds all exclaim, 'The flowers full-blown all plucked have been, to-morrow we the same!' 6. Want ( māṅg ); need; necessity. 7. Scarcity; drought; dearth.
s. f. Bawl, calling out aloud, cry, call.

ety: pukār˚ = S.स्फूत्कार or फूत्कार; Prk. पुक्कार˚
def: v.t. To call, call out to, to hail; to invoke; to invite; to summon; to publish, proclaim; to sue; to term, to denominate; — v.n. To call out, call alond; to cry (one's wares), to shout, bawl, to cry out, to exclaim; to lodge a complaint, to complain; to be called (as a roll-call)
more: v. n. 1. To call (بلانا 1, 2). 2. To cry ones wares. 3. To call aloud; bawl; cry out; exclaim; shout; yell; cry for help. 1. Māṅ māre, aur māṅ hī māṅ pukāre . Prov. The mother beats her child, the child still 'mother mother' cries. (The dog licks the hand that beats him). 2. Pagṛī bhī ādmī kī utāre hai ādmī, Chillā-ke ādmī ko pukāre hai ādmī . Man. Nazir. Who turbans make off with are also men, And men to whom for help cry other men. 3. Chhāḍo merā achrā maiṅ gārī dūṅgī, Sās, nanad ko pukār hī lūṅgī . Song. Thumrī. Let go my mantle's hem, I say, I will abuse you straight, I'll scream out to my mother-in-law and sister of my mate. 4. To invoke; call on God. 1. Nayā Musalmān 'Allāh hī Allāh' pukāre! Prov. A Musalmān just made! 'Allah, Allāh!' he cries all day. 2. Mohan rasyā āë bagyā meṅ, phūl rahī sab kalī kalī , Dohā. Koī kalī Hari nām japat hai, koī pukāre 'Ali, Alī .' The amorous Mohan…
v. a. To call aloud, to bawl, to cry out, exclaim, shout.
subs: v.t. To call, &c.; to publish, proclaim.

s.f. Cakes, or pieces of bread, which are laid over dishes when bringing to table, and under which the meat is kept warm; cakes imbued with ghī or clarified butter (from being laid over certain greasy dishes, as do-piyāza, &c.: These cakes are very soft, rich, and savoury; and hence māmā puḵẖtariyāṅ khānā, 'to eat rich meal cakes prepared by his mother,' is a phrase employed to denote one who is accustomed to luxury, and incapable of labour or fatigue).
more: n. f. Wom. Rich meal cakes. Jis ne māmā pakhtaryāṅ khā/?/īṅ, us se kām nahīṅ hotā .

s. f. 1. Cakes (or bread) which are laid over dishes when bringing to table, and under which the meat is kept warm. 2. Cakes imbued with clarified butter from being laid over certain greasy dishes, such as dopiyāza, &c. (These cakes are very soft and savoury; whence) ماماپختريان کهانا māmā puḵẖtariyāṅ khānā, To eat cakes of this description prepared by his mother, is applied to a person accustomed to luxury and incapable of labour or fatigue.

def: s.m. A bridge; a causeway, an embankment; — pul bāṅdhnā, v.n. To make a bridge (over, par), to bridge; to make an embankment or dike, to limit; to provide an abundant supply (of), to supply in abundance, to form a heap, to heap
more: n. m. A bridge. pul bāṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To make a bridge. 2. To raise a dam against inundation. 3. To supply abundantly; heap; cram. pul sarāt , n. m. Mah. A bridge along which on the day of judgment the righteous will pass to heaven, and the wicked fall into hell. pul-bandī , n. f. Keeping bridges or embankments in repair; a tax imposed for the purpose. pul ṭūṭ'nā , v. n. 1. To break (a bridge). 2. To burst forth in excess; to crowd.
s. m. A bridge, an embankment. پل باندهنا pul bāndhnā, v. a. ( lit. To make a bridge) 1. To limit. 2. To abound.
subs: s.f. Embankment; keeping bridges or embankments in repair; a tax levied for this purpose; — sodomy
(-kī), To commit sodomy; to ravish, violate; — pul ṭūṭnā, v.n. 'Bridge to break'; to burst or come forth en masse, to crowd
s.m. A bridge over which (in the belief of Mohammadans) the righteous will pass into Paradise, and from which the wicked fall into hell, on the day of judgment.

Kartā hai sair koī koṭhī kā le sahārā, Muflis bhī kar rahā hai pūle tale guzārā. Barsāt . Rains. Nazīr. Some pacing in high airy halls you saw, While poor men hugged the shelter of some straw.

s. m. A bundle or truss of grass or straw. پولي تلي گذران کرنا pūle tale guẕrān karnā (lit. to live under a bundle of straw) implies a very destitute condition.

def: adj. (f. ī), Full, complete, perfect; satisfied; fulfilled, accomplished; entire, whole, total, undivided, undiminished, unbroken, uncut; exact, precise, fitting, coinciding, just, sufficient, enough; true, standard, up to the mark; faithful, constant, firm; old, ripe, experienced, skilled in; powerful
more: adj. S. पूर्ण 1. Full; brimful; up to the ears or brim. 2. Coinciding; fitting; exact; precise. 1. Pūrā to tolo! 2. Jahāṅ kharch nahīṅ, wahāṅ har ek gāṅṭh kā pūrā . Prov. Where naught's to pay full pockets sway. (Offers of money are plentiful when it is not wanted, but when it is wanted every pocket is empty). 3. Whole; unbroken; uncut; undivided. Pūrā āṅkh . A whole number. 4. Old; advanced in years; experienced. Kachchā to kachorī māṅge, pūrī māṅge pūrā, Non mirach to Kāith māṅge, Bāman māṅge būrā . Prov. The young ones want crisp cakes, the old ones soft sponge-cakes, The (imbibing) Kāith wants pepper and salt, the Brahman sugar takes. 5. Entire; complete; perfect. 1. Pure haiṅ wohī mard jo har hāl meṅ khush haiṅ . Nazīr. The perfect man is happy under all circumstances. 2. Shakkar kā jin-ke halwā huā, woh to pūre haiṅ, Guṛ kā huā hai jinke woh un se adhūre haiṅ . Nazīr. Who sugar in …
n. m. A kind of sweet cake. Tatte, tatte pūṛe kar de, rī māṅ? Hāth jale, mūṅh mīṭhā, rī māṅ! Girls' song. Hot hot cakes now make me, Ma! fingers-burning hot, mouth-sweetening, Ma!
adj. Full, complete, entire, exact, perfect, sufficient, just, total, ripe, powerful. پوراکرنا pūrā karnā, v. a. To fill, to fulfil, to accomplish, to complete; to reimburse.
subs: v.n. To turn out up to the mark, to come out (of an ordeal) with credit, to pass a satisfactory examination, to succeed
s.m. Stamp paper of full value
s.f. (in Gram.) A complete sentence, a proposition
(or pūrī) parṇā, v.n. To be enough, to suffice
adj. Gone the full time, gone nine months with child; — pūre din lagnā, v.n. To approach the full period of gestation (a woman), to enter on the ninth month of pregnancy
v.t. To fill, fill up; to satisfy; to end, complete, finish; to accomplish, to fulfil, carry out, act up to, keep (a promise); to reimburse; to make up (a deficiency).

pūre āsamān mẽ rākh phail gayī thī
Ash had spread across the whole sky.

adj. & s.m. Filling, completing; filling up; satisfying; full, &c. ( = pūrā, q.v.); — that which fills or completes; — (in Arith.) the multiplier; — a cake of meal offered at the conclusion of the funeral rites or oblation to the manes; — closing the right nostril and drawing up air through the left (as a religious exercise); — a citron, Citrus medica.
n. m. A man ( purush ).
more: n. f. परीक्षा = परि + ईक्ष to see. 1. Test; proof; trial; examination. 2. Scrutiny; discrimination; judgment; intelligence; skill. 1. Inko achchhī parakh hai . 2. Lohe ke chane dāṅt tale pānī haiṅ usko, Khāyā woh nahīṅ jātā, par khāte * haiṅ usko. Dānāī se dāṅt us pe lagātā nahīṅ koī, Sab usko bhunāte † haiṅ, par khātā nahīṅ koī : Riddle. Ans. A rupes. Though iron hard it turns as water soft between the teeth, Eat it you really cant, and yet all people eat therewith, No need the knowing have between their teeth to place this thing, All roast or break it, but not one will to his mouth it bring.
s. f. Inspection, examination, trial, proof, experiment.
wety: From Sanskrit पुरुष (púruṣa). Compare Punjabi پرکھ (purakh).

def: v.t. To fill; to fulfil; to complete, finish.
more: of old. 1. Ancient ( qadīm ); antique; of yore. 2. Experienced ( tajruba-kār ); veteran; knowing. 3. Old-fashioned; unfashionable; antiquated; out of date; obsolete. 4. Old; stale; faded; used up; passè ; time-worn; worn out; decayed. Nayā nau din, purānā sau din . Prov. New for nine days, old for a hundred. (Said of a wife or a kept mistress). 5. Old; aged ( buḍḍhā ); decrepit; wrinkled. Mūṅh ko dhone meṅ dugānā mal-ke kar detī hai surkh, Par ye raunaq par purānā ṭhīkrā ātā nahīṅ . Wom. Rāhat. My twin-love scrapes and scrapes her face to make it red, But old bricks cant be furbished up and youthful made. purānā , n. m. ( Slang ). An old catamite.
v. a. To fulfil; carry out; complete; finish.
1. ( पुराण ) adj. Old. 2. (from r. पूर Fill) v. a. To fill.

def: adj. & s.m. Belonging to ancient or olden times, ancient, old, aged, primeval; worn out, laid aside; — a thing of the past, past event; a tale of the past, ancient history legendary and traditionary, legend; the name given to certain well-known sacred works (supposed to have been compiled by the poet Vyāsa, and comprising the whole body of modern Hindū theology and mythology. There are eighteen acknowledged purāṇas).
more: n. m. S. पुरा of old. A purān or sacred poem, supposed to have been complied or composed by Vyāsā . There are eighteen acknowledged Purāns , comprizing the whole body of Hindu theology. Each Purān treats especially of five topics, i. e. the creation; the destruction and renovation of worlds; the genealogy of gods and heroes; the reigns of the Manus; and the work of their descendants.

n. m. S. प्र, अण् to breathe. 1. The breath of life; life; soul. Ek tiryā, deh se nyārī, Raste bich paṛī bechārī, Āṅkh, nāk, mukh us-ke aṅg, Pirān gaë par pī ke saṅg . Riddle. Ans. Keṅchlī . The cast-off skin of a snake. A female with no body see, Outstretched upon the road is she, Eyes, nose, and mouth for body sole, Fled with her lover is her soul. 2. One beloved; sweetheart; mistress. pirān-asthān , n. m. A vital part of the body. pirān-bidyā , n. m. Psychology. pirān pratishṭhā , n. f. Imparting life to the image of a divinity by certain spells. pirān chhuṭānā , Hin. v. a. 1. To separate soul and body; to kill; despatch. 2. To save one's life; to escape with life. B/?/ṛī mushkil se pirān chhuṭāë haiṅ. pirān chhūṭnā, yā nikalnā , v. n. 1. To breathe one's last; depart this life. 2. s. m. A Purāṇa or sacred and poetical work, supposed to have been compiled or composed by the poet Vyāsa. There are eighteen acknowledged Purāṇas, comprising the whole body of Hindū theology: and, each Purāṇa treats of five topicks especially, i. e. the creation; the destruction and renovation of worlds; the genealogy of gods and heroes; the reigns of the Manus; and, the transactions of their descendants. The Purāṇas are, 1. Brahma; 2. Padma, or the lotus; 3. Brahmāṇḍa, or the egg of Brahmā; 4. Agni, or fire; 5. Vish̤ṇu; 6. Garuḍa, the bird or vehicle of Vish̤ṇu; 7. Brahmavaivarta, or transformations of Brahmā; 8. Siva; 9. Linga; 10. Nārada, son of Brahmā; 11. Skanda, son of Siva; 12. Mārkaṇḍeya, so called from a sage of that name; 13. Bhavish̤yat, future or prophetick; 14. Matsya, or the fish; 15. Varāha, or boar; 16. Kūrma, or tortoise; 17. Vāmana, or dwarf; and 18. The Bhāgavat, or li…
